perturbation.original_text: 13. Remedies . The Company and you each hereby acknowledge and agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for breach of this Agreement by you or any of your Representatives or for breach of this Agreement by the Company or any of its Representatives and that therefore, both the Company and you shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunctions and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach without necessity of posting any bond or other security, and without proof of any actual damages. Such remedies shall nonetheless not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement, and shall be in addition to all other remedies available to you and to the Company at law or equity. In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to receive from the other party the reasonable legal fees and expenses incurred by the prevailing party in connection with such litigation, including any appeal therefrom.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. Remedies . The Company and you each hereby acknowledge and agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for breach of this Agreement by you or any of your Representatives or for breach of this Agreement by the Company or any of its Representatives and that therefore, both the Company and you shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunctions and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach without necessity of posting any bond or other security, and without proof of any actual damages. Such remedies shall nonetheless not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement, and shall be in addition to all other remedies available to you and to the Company at law or equity.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the clause 'In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to receive from the other party the reasonable legal fees and expenses incurred by the prevailing party in connection with such litigation, including any appeal therefrom.' eliminates a standard provision for fee recovery, potentially violating the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing under Delaware law, which requires parties to act honestly and reasonably in the performance and enforcement of contracts.  By removing this provision, the agreement introduces ambiguity and could unfairly disadvantage a party forced to litigate a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing under Delaware law.
perturbation.law_explanation: Delaware law implies a covenant of good faith and fair dealing in every contract. Removing the prevailing party clause could be interpreted as acting in bad faith, particularly if one party is significantly disadvantaged in a dispute and cannot recover legal fees, thus discouraging legitimate claims and defenses.  The covenant requires parties to act honestly and reasonably, and this omission could be viewed as an attempt to circumvent that obligation by creating a financial barrier to justice.

General Provisions Part 3 Territorial Applicability and General Rules Â§ 1-301. Territorial applicability; partiesâ power to choose applicable law. (a) Except as otherwise provided in this section, when a transaction bears a reasonable relation to this State and also to another state or nation the parties may agree that the law either of this State or of such other state or nation shall govern their rights and duties. (b) In the absence of an agreement effective under subsection (a), and except as provided in subsection (c), the Uniform Commercial Code applies to transactions bearing an appropriate relation to this state. (c) If one of the following provisions of the Uniform Commercial Code specifies the applicable law, that provision governs and a contrary agreement is effective only to the extent permitted by the law so specified: (1) Section 2-402; (2) Sections 2A-105 and 2A-106; (3) Section 4-102; (4) Section 4A-507; (5) Section 5-116; (6) Section 8-110; (7) Sections 9-301 through 9-307; (8) Section 12-107. 5A Del. (b) The obligations of good faith, diligence, reasonableness, and care prescribed by the Uniform Commercial Code may not be disclaimed by agreement. The parties, by agreement, may determine the standards by which the performance of those obliga
perturbation.justification: The original contract included a clause awarding legal fees and expenses to the prevailing party in litigation. The changed text removes this clause. The Perturbation Explanation states this could violate the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing under Delaware law. The Law Explanation states removing the clause could be interpreted as acting in bad faith. Scraped Law Snippet 1, though not directly on point, generally discusses the application and variation of laws through agreement, but notes obligations of good faith may not be disclaimed. By removing the fee recovery clause, a party forced to litigate a breach may be significantly disadvantaged if they cannot recover legal fees, which could be viewed as an attempt to circumvent the obligation of good faith, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Evaluation Material and Confidentiality . You and your Representatives shall use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of considering, evaluating and negotiating a Transaction, shall not use any of the Evaluation Material for any other purpose, shall keep the Evaluation Material strictly confidential, and, except as provided in this Section 2 and in Section 4 , shall not disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever without the prior written consent of the Company; provided, however, that the Evaluation Material may be disclosed to your Representatives who need to know the information so disclosed for the purpose of evaluating or advising you with respect to considering, evaluating and negotiating a Transaction, and who are directed by you to keep such Evaluation Material confidential and who are provided with a copy of this Agreement. You shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of your Representatives, and you agree, at your sole expense, to take all commercially reasonable measures to assure that your Representatives do not make any prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or use (including in legal proceedings to the extent set forth in Section 4 of this Agreement) of the Evaluation Material. Without limiting the foregoing, neither you nor any of your affiliates or Representatives shall use any information obtained from the Evaluation Material to divert or attempt to divert any business or customer of the Company, or otherwise use any such information competitively against the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Evaluation Material and Confidentiality . You and your Representatives shall use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of considering, evaluating and negotiating a Transaction, shall not use any of the Evaluation Material for any other purpose, shall keep the Evaluation Material strictly confidential, and, except as provided in this Section 2 and in Section 4 , shall not disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever without the prior written consent of the Company; provided, however, that the Evaluation Material may be disclosed to your Representatives who need to know the information so disclosed for the purpose of evaluating or advising you with respect to considering, evaluating and negotiating a Transaction, and who are directed by you to keep such Evaluation Material confidential. You shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of your Representatives, and you agree, at your sole expense, to take all commercially reasonable measures to assure that your Representatives do not make any prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or use (including in legal proceedings to the extent set forth in Section 4 of this Agreement) of the Evaluation Material. Without limiting the foregoing, neither you nor any of your affiliates or Representatives shall use any information obtained from the Evaluation Material to divert or attempt to divert any business or customer of the Company, or otherwise use any such information competitively against the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'and who are provided with a copy of this Agreement' from the conditions under which evaluation material may be disclosed to representatives creates an issue. The agreement now doesn't explicitly mandate that representatives receive a copy of the NDA, which could violate Delaware's trade secret protection laws. The omitted provision weakens the explicit measures taken to maintain confidentiality, impacting the protection afforded under Delaware law.
perturbation.law_explanation: The omission weakens the steps taken to ensure confidentiality and could jeopardize trade secret status under the DUTSA. If the representatives are not explicitly provided with the agreement, establishing the 'reasonable efforts' necessary to maintain secrecy under the DUTSA becomes more difficult, potentially leading to the loss of trade secret protection for the disclosed information.

Derives independent economic value, actual or potential, from not being generally known to, and not b
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the explicit requirement that representatives receive a copy of the agreement, which weakens the measures to ensure confidentiality. The Law Explanation states that this omission makes it more difficult to establish the 'reasonable efforts' necessary to maintain secrecy under the DUTSA, potentially leading to the loss of trade secret protection. Scraped Law Snippet 1 defines 'Misappropriation' as the 'Disclosure or use of a trade secret of another without express or implied consent'. By not ensuring that representatives receive a copy of the confidentiality agreement, the company's ability to prove reasonable efforts to maintain secrecy is undermined, increasing the risk of misappropriation under Delaware law. The omission contradicts the implicit requirement of taking reasonable measures to protect trade secrets, leading to a higher risk of losing trade secret status and violating the Delaware Code regarding trade secrets.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Securities Law Compliance . It is expected that the Evaluation Material will contain material information about the Company that has not been disclosed by the Company to the public generally. You hereby acknowledge that you are aware, and you agree to advise your Representatives who are informed as to the matters that are the subject of this Agreement, that the U.S. federal and many state securities laws prohibit any person who has received from an issuer material, non-public information concerning the matters that are the subject of this Agreement from purchasing or selling securities of such issuer or from communicating such information to any person under circumstances in which it is foreseeable that such person is reasonably likely to purchase or sell such securities.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Securities Law Compliance . It is expected that the Evaluation Material will contain material information about the Company that has not been disclosed by the Company to the public generally. You hereby acknowledge that you are aware that the U.S. federal and many state securities laws prohibit any person who has received from an issuer material, non-public information concerning the matters that are the subject of this Agreement from purchasing or selling securities of such issuer or from communicating such information to any person under circumstances in which it is foreseeable that such person is reasonably likely to purchase or sell such securities.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of 'and you agree to advise your Representatives who are informed as to the matters that are the subject of this Agreement' weakens the obligation to ensure representatives understand insider trading rules. While the recipient acknowledges awareness, they are no longer explicitly required to inform their representatives. This could violate SEC Rule 10b5-1, which requires companies to demonstrate reasonable efforts to prevent insider trading. Failure to explicitly require advising representatives weakens these efforts.
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 10b5-1 aims to prevent insider trading. By removing the explicit requirement to advise representatives, the company's efforts to ensure compliance are weakened. Although the primary party acknowledges awareness, the SEC could argue that the agreement fails to implement adequate procedures to prevent representatives from engaging in illegal trading activities based on the confidential information received.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 240.10b5-1 - Trading “on the basis of” material nonpublic information in insider trading cases. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.10b5-1 - Trading “on the basis of” material nonpublic information in insider trading cases. CFR prev | next § 240.10b5-1 Trading “on the basis of” material nonpublic information in insider trading cases. (a) Manipulative or deceptive devices. The “manipulative or deceptive device[s] or contrivance[s]” prohibited by Section 10(b) of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78j ) and § 240.10b-5 (Rule 10b-5) thereunder include, among other things, the purchase or sale of a security of any issuer , on the basis of material nonpublic information about that security or issuer , in breach of a duty of trust or confidence that is owed directly, indirectly, or derivatively, to the issuer of that security or the shareholders of that issuer , or to any other person who is the source of the material nonpublic information. (b) Awareness of material nonpublic information. Subject to the affirmative defenses in paragraph (c) of this section , a purchase or sale of a security of an issuer is on the basis of material nonpublic information for purposes of Section 10(b) and Rule 10b-5 if the person making the purchase or sale was aware of the material nonpublic information when the person made the purchase or sale. The law of insider trading is otherwise defined by judicial opinions construing Rule 10b-5, and Rule 10b5-1 does not modify the scope of insider trading law in any other respect. (c) Affirmative defenses. (1)(i) Subject to paragraph (1)(ii) of this section , a person's purchase or sale is not on the basis of material nonpublic information if the person making the purchase or sale demonstrates that: (A) Before becoming aware of the information, the person had: (1) Entered into a binding contract to purchase or sell the security , (2) Instruc
perturbation.justification: The omission in the changed text removes the explicit requirement for the recipient to advise their representatives about securities law compliance. The original text ensures that not only the recipient but also their representatives are informed about and adhere to insider trading regulations. The changed text weakens this obligation, potentially leading to representatives unknowingly violating securities laws. Snippet 2 discusses manipulative or deceptive devices prohibited by Section 10(b) of the Act (15 U.S.C. 78j) and § 240.10b-5 (Rule 10b-5). While it does not explicitly state the need to inform representatives, the perturbation undermines the intent of preventing insider trading by potentially leaving representatives uninformed. The omission directly weakens the effort to prevent insider trading, creating a contradiction with the spirit and objective of Rule 10b5-1.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Termination of Discussions at Any Time Return of Evaluation Material . At any time upon the request of the Company for any reason, you shall (and will direct your Representatives to) promptly, and in any event no later than 10 days after receipt of the request, deliver to the Company, or at your option, destroy, all Evaluation Material (including all copies, extracts and other reproductions thereof, whether in paper, electronic or other form or media) furnished to you or your Representatives by or on behalf of the Company pursuant to this Agreement; provided, however, that you and your Representatives shall be entitled to retain one complete copy, in electronic archival storage form, of all Evaluation Material in accordance with document retention laws or regulations applicable to you and to such other persons, as the case may be, but only to the extent that appropriate personnel whose primary function within your organization and within the organizations of such other persons, as the case may be, is information technology or compliance in nature will have unrestricted access to such retained information; and provided, further, however, that your legal counsel will be entitled to retain one complete copy of Evaluation Material in paper format as may be necessary to document your consideration of a Transaction for the purpose of establishing compliance with any applicable laws or regulations and for defending and maintaining any litigation (or any arbitral or administrative case or proceeding) relating to this Agreement or the Evaluation Material; provided, that all such information shall continue to be kept confidential and shall be stored only in counsel’s record archives to which access is not made generally available. The destruction of the Evaluation Material, including that prepared by you or your Representatives, shall be certified in writing to the Company by an authorized representative supervising such destruction. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, you and your Representatives shall continue to be bound by your and their confidentiality obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Termination of Discussions at Any Time Return of Evaluation Material . At any time upon the request of the Company for any reason, you shall (and will direct your Representatives to) promptly, and in any event no later than 10 days after receipt of the request, deliver to the Company, or at your option, destroy, all Evaluation Material (including all copies, extracts and other reproductions thereof, whether in paper, electronic or other form or media) furnished to you or your Representatives by or on behalf of the Company pursuant to this Agreement; provided, however, that you and your Representatives shall be entitled to retain one complete copy, in electronic archival storage form, of all Evaluation Material in accordance with document retention laws or regulations applicable to you and to such other persons, as the case may be, but only to the extent that appropriate personnel whose primary function within your organization and within the organizations of such other persons, as the case may be, is information technology or compliance in nature will have unrestricted access to such retained information; and provided, further, however, that your legal counsel will be entitled to retain one complete copy of Evaluation Material in paper format as may be necessary to document your consideration of a Transaction for the purpose of establishing compliance with any applicable laws or regulations and for defending and maintaining any litigation (or any arbitral or administrative case or proceeding) relating to this Agreement or the Evaluation Material. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, you and your Representatives shall continue to be bound by your and their confidentiality obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the clause 'The destruction of the Evaluation Material, including that prepared by you or your Representatives, shall be certified in writing to the Company by an authorized representative supervising such destruction.' eliminates the required certification of destruction, which could be seen as weakening the measures to protect confidential information. Under Delaware law, if the company fails to maintain reasonable measures to protect its trade secrets, it may be argued there's been a waiver of rights.
perturbation.law_explanation: The removal of the certification requirement diminishes the 'reasonable efforts' taken to protect trade secrets, potentially violating the DUTSA. Without a formal certification process, proving that the recipient adequately destroyed the material becomes challenging, and the company's trade secret protection might be at risk.

perturbation.justification: The original contract requires a written certification of destruction of evaluation material by an authorized representative. The modified contract omits this requirement. The Perturbation Explanation suggests this omission weakens protection of confidential information, potentially leading to a waiver of rights under Delaware law. The Law Explanation asserts the removal reduces 'reasonable efforts' to protect trade secrets, potentially violating DUTSA. Scraped Law Snippet 1 defines 'Misappropriation' to include acquisition of a trade secret by improper means or disclosure/use without consent, implying a need for reasonable measures to protect trade secrets. Without the certification, proving adequate destruction becomes difficult. Therefore, the omission of the certification requirement in the changed text directly contradicts the need to maintain reasonable measures to safeguard trade secrets, thus creating a legal contradiction.
